foundation.title: CLASS
foundation.subtitle: Object Blueprints
foundation.content: A Class is a recipe or blueprint for an 'Object'. It group together data (Variables) and actions (Methods) into a single entity.
foundation.scenario: A Blueprints for a Car. It defines 'Color' (Data) and 'Drive' (Action). You don't drive the blueprint, you build an actual car from it.

examples.title: The Blueprint
examples.explanation: Creating a new class structure using the 'class' keyword.

examples.title: Self Param
examples.explanation: Using 'self' to refer back to the specific instance being used.

examples.title: Grouping
examples.explanation: Keeping all related data and logic in a single logical namespace.

foundation.title: CONSTRUCT
foundation.subtitle: Birth Methods
foundation.content: The __init__ method is the constructor. It's the first bit of code that runs exactly when an object is 'born' (created).
foundation.scenario: A New Player character appearing in a game. The 'init' sets their base Level to 1 and their inventory to empty instantly.

examples.title: Initialization
examples.explanation: Setting the starting state for any object created from this blueprint.

examples.title: Default Value
examples.explanation: Setting a value that every single object starts with automatically.

examples.title: The Spawn
examples.explanation: The moment you call the class name to bring a new instance to life.

foundation.title: INHERIT
foundation.subtitle: Parent/Child
foundation.content: Inheritance allows one class to 'steal' the features of another class and then add its own unique powers on top.
foundation.scenario: A Parent and Child. The Child (Subclass) has the same eye color (Features) as the Parent (Superclass) but can also play guitar (Special Power).

examples.title: Basic Link
examples.explanation: Creating a subclass that takes everything from a main base class.

examples.title: Overriding
examples.explanation: Changing how a inherited method behaves in the specific child class.

examples.title: Super Case
examples.explanation: Calling the parent's code from inside the child to build on top.
